meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Overcome 'No Project Ideas' (Advanced Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with project indecision. Use this advanced SaaS starter kit guide with Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first production-ready app.
content.answer_block: The 'no project idea' paralysis is a structural failure, not a creative one. Stop searching for the 'perfect' idea and start building a modular SaaS starter kit. By mastering architectural patterns, authentication, and database schemas, you transform your workflow into a repeatable engine that makes any app idea instantly executable.
content.intro:
The blank editor window is the graveyard of ambition. You’ve spent weeks scrolling through forums, paralyzed by the phantom need for a 'billion-dollar' concept, while your actual coding skills atrophy. The reality of professional development isn't waiting for a lightning bolt of inspiration; it's about having the infrastructure ready to capture and iterate on reality when it strikes.

Today, we stop building 'projects' and start building a platform. We are going to architect a robust, production-grade SaaS starter kit. Using Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for intelligent, automated implementation, you will transition from a student struggling to find a purpose to an engineer who can manifest a functional application in an afternoon.

steps.title: Define the Modular Architecture
steps.description: Establish a clean separation between your authentication, database, and UI layers so your kit remains reusable for any future SaaS project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a modular SaaS architecture using Next.js 15, Drizzle ORM, and Auth.js. Ensure the project structure separates services, components, and hooks for maximum scalability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize a project structure with clean architectural boundaries for a reusable SaaS starter kit.'

steps.title: Implement Intelligent Auth Flow
steps.description: Don't reinvent the wheel. Configure a secure, role-based access control (RBAC) system that you can drop into any future application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an authentication service using Auth.js with Google and GitHub providers, including middleware for protected routes and database schema for user profiles.

steps.title: Database Schema Automation
steps.description: Create a flexible schema that supports multi-tenancy from day one, allowing your kit to handle multiple users or organizations seamlessly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Drizzle schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application, including tables for organizations, memberships, and subscription tiers.

steps.title: Build the Component Library
steps.description: Standardize your UI with a set of core components (buttons, inputs, modals) that ensure brand consistency across every app you build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a UI component library using Shadcn/UI and Tailwind CSS, focused on accessibility and dark-mode support.

steps.title: Deploy the 'Engine'
steps.description: Automate your CI/CD pipeline so that every time you commit, your starter kit is ready for production deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and Vercel deployment of the starter kit.

pro_tips: Always build for multi-tenancy from day one; retrofitting it later is the #1 cause of failed SaaS refactors.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your auth middleware; it is the most critical security layer in your kit.
Treat your starter kit like a product; maintain a CHANGELOG.md file to track which features you've perfected over time.
Don't hardcode environment variables; use a strictly typed Zod configuration schema to validate env vars at runtime.
Focus on 'Time to First Commit'—the faster your kit scaffolds, the more iterations you can perform on your actual business logic.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is functional, build three 'micro-SaaS' apps using it this month. Focus on solving one tiny, specific problem for each to test your kit's modularity.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer a student waiting for an idea; you are an architect with the tools to build the future. Stop waiting for permission and start building your legacy. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
